metadata.description: Complete mapping between rgbif R functions and GBIF API endpoints
metadata.note: Some functions map to multiple API endpoints. occ_download_describe covers 6 format-specific endpoints; name_usage covers 17 endpoints via the data parameter; name_parse supports both GET and POST methods.
metadata.priority_field: Optional 'priority' field can be added to mappings and ignored_endpoints. Values: 'high' (critical/commonly used), 'low' (nice-to-have), 'unclassified' (new/not yet prioritized). Field can be omitted if not classified.
metadata.parameter_mappings_field: Optional 'parameter_mappings' object maps GBIF API parameter names to rgbif parameter names when they differ (e.g., {'gbifId': 'key'}). Used to avoid false positives in missing parameter detection.

general.curlopts: Internal parameter for curl options - accepted by all functions
Variadic arguments - accepted by some functions for flexibility

general.Accept-Language: HTTP header for language preference - not implemented in rgbif

notes: This mapping includes all 72 API-calling functions in rgbif
Some functions like dataset, nodes, organizations, installations, networks use a 'data' parameter that modifies behavior
Export functions (collection_export, institution_export, lit_export, dataset_export) use the same base endpoints as search
Prep functions (occ_download_prep, derived_dataset_prep) validate but don't execute API calls
Count functions (occ_count, lit_count) are wrappers that set limit=0
- Some rgbif functions use more R-friendly parameter names than the GBIF API
- When checking for missing parameters, mapped parameters are considered covered
1. New GBIF API endpoints are released
3. Function parameters change to match API updates
4. Parameter name mappings change or new ones are added
